ogTitle: Salmon Gums vs Samson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Salmon Gums and Samson.
heading: Salmon Gums vs Samson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Salmon Gums, Western Australia and Samson, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Salmon Gums has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Samson (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Salmon Gums or Samson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Salmon Gums has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Samson scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Salmon Gums scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Samson scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Salmon Gums or Samson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Salmon Gums has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
